# rome

rome is a minimal R package to read and write multiscale OME-Zarr files.

It also provides helper and methods to manipulate the resulting
`ome_zarr` objects the same way one would manipulate traditional arrays
in R. For example, you can subset an `ome_zarr` object using the `[`
operator, and the subsetting will be applied to all levels of the
multiscale OME-Zarr object.

## Installation

You can install the development version of rome like so:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("Huber-group-EMBL/rome")
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to solve a common problem:

``` r
library(rome)
x <- ome_read(
  system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.4", "10501752.zarr", package = "rome")
)
plot(x, all = TRUE)
```

![](reference/figures/README-example-1.png)

# Package index

## Validate, read & write OME-Zarr data in R

- [`ome_read()`](https://huber-group-embl.github.io/rome/reference/ome_read.md)
  : Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file
- [`ome_validate()`](https://huber-group-embl.github.io/rome/reference/ome_validate.md)
  : Validate a multiscale OME-Zarr file

## Manipulating `ome_zarr` objects

- [`plot(`*`<ome_zarr>`*`)`](https://huber-group-embl.github.io/rome/reference/plot.ome_zarr.md)
  :

  Plot an `ome_zarr` object.

- [`` `[`( ``*`<ome_zarr>`*`)`](https://huber-group-embl.github.io/rome/reference/sub-.ome_zarr.md)
  :

  Subset an `ome-zarr` object

- [`extract_levels()`](https://huber-group-embl.github.io/rome/reference/extract_levels.md)
  :

  Extract specific levels from a multiscale `ome-zarr` object
